Q1.label: Question 1
Q1.scenario: Company B is a health and care insurance company that has written Private Medical Insurance (PMI) business for many years in Country Y. Company B calculates its statutory reserves on a Solvency Il basis. The marketing director of Company B has proposed introducing a new annual health check option for its PMI business. The health check is a 30-minute in person appointment with a healthcare professional and involves a range of health tests and questions. Policyholders who take the annual health check option will also be given a wearable electronic device that will record data about the policyholder such as daily step counts, heart rate, calories burned and weight. The policyholder will be able to upload the data from their electronic device to their smartphone or computer to monitor their health. The information collected by the policyholder's wearable device will be shared with Company B. Policyholders who take up the health check option and use their wearable electronic device will receive a discount on their PMI premiums. The policyholder's premium would not be directly affected by the health data provided from the health checks. There will be no charge to the policyholder for the annual health check, but there will be a small upfront charge to cover the cost of the wearable device. The marketing director believes that the introduction of annual health checks and wearable electronic devices will lead to better health outcomes for the policyholder as it will encourage policyholders to lead a healthier lifestyle. The marketing director believes that Company B will also benefit from healthier policyholders.

(i).question: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of the marketing director's proposal for:
(i).synopsis: PMI Health Checks, Wearable Devices, Premium Discounts

(a).question: Company B.
(a).synopsis: Corporate Strategy, Health Outcomes, Marketing Proposals

(b).question: policyholders. The chief actuary of Company B has asked about the potential impact of introducing the annual health check on Company B 's statutory reserving assumptions for its PMI business.
(b).synopsis: Statutory Reserving, Data Sharing, Wearable Tech Impact

(ii).question:
Discuss the impact of introducing the annual health check on the valuation assumptions for Company B 's Solvency Il Best Estimate Liabilities for its

PMI business.  Company B has been approached by an external third-party company that has offered to carry out the annual health assessments and collect and monitor the data from the wearable devices. The third-party company has carried out similar services in another country and is currently setting up a number of medical centres in Country Y. The annual health assessment will be carried out in person at one of the third-party company's medical centres in Country Y.

SAI S2025-3
(ii).synopsis: Solvency II, Best Estimate Liabilities, Valuation Assumptions

(iii).question: Outline the factors Company B should consider in deciding whether to use the third-party company.  Company B agreed to implement the health check option and outsourced the administration of the health check option to the third-party company. Three years after the health check option was added the management of Company B carried out an analysis of the experience of PMI business since the health check option was added. The management of Company B are pleased with the take up of the option, and the level of new business sales. However new business profitability for its PMI business has been significantly lower than expectations. The finance director of Company B has proposed that Company B should introduce an annual charge for the health check for both existing and new business.
(iii).synopsis: Third-party Outsourcing, Operational Due Diligence, Experience Analysis

(iv).question: Discuss the finance director's proposal.
(iv).synopsis: Post-implementation Review, Charging for Health Checks, Profitability Gap

Q2.label: Question 2
Q2.scenario:
Country A has a small private Long Term Care (LTC) insurance market that has been slow to develop over the past 15 years. Country A has an aging population and a publicly funded health system with no private provision of healthcare services. Access to government funded long term care is means-tested. The majority, 75% of the adult population in Country A, self-fund their long term care needs. Country B has private provision of both healthcare and long term care services, with no public provision of these services. Private LTC insurance was launched in Country B 15 years ago, and since then the market has grown and is now well established with
75% of the adult population purchasing some level of private LTC insurance cover.

(i).question: above.
(i).synopsis: LTC Market Development, Aging Population, Means-tested Care

(ii).question: Contrast the main product features of immediate needs LTC insurance to those shown in part
(ii).synopsis: Immediate Needs LTC, Product Features, Market Comparison

(iii).question: Describe possible reasons why the LTC insurance market may be more developed in Country B than Country A.  The government of Country A is concerned that its aging population will mean more of its citizens will have insufficient funds to meet the costs of long-term care and that an increasing proportion of the population will require financial support from the government to meet the cost of their long term care. The government of Country A is therefore considering encouraging insurance companies based outside of Country A to sell LTC insurance in Country A. Company XYZ is the market leading health and care insurance company for LTC insurance in Country B. The government of Country A has asked several health and care insurance companies based outside of Country A, including Company XYZ, to start selling LTC insurance in Country A. Company XYZ's main LTC product provides benefits on an indemnity basis in return for annual premiums payable until care is required. Company XYZ is considering if it will start writing pre-funded LTC insurance in Country A.
(iii).synopsis: LTC Market Penetration, Regulatory Encouragement, Foreign Insurers

(iv).question:
Suggest possible reasons why Company XYZ should start selling pre-funded

LTC business in Country A.
(iv).synopsis: Pre-funded LTC, Strategic Expansion, Market Entry

(v).question: Discuss the factors Company XYZ should consider in determining if it is appropriate to price LTC business sold in Country A using the same pricing basis as is used for LTC business in Country B.
(v).synopsis: Pricing Basis, Cross-border Factors, LTC Assumptions

Q3.label: Question 3
Q3.scenario: Company A is a large health and care insurance company in Country Z. Company A specialises in, and sells large volumes of, individual Critical Illness (CI) business.

(i).question:
Define the term 'asset share'.  Company A has recently reviewed its existing business and found that longer duration policies (i.e. policies that have been in force for 10 years or more) written on guaranteed premium terms, have an asset share that is greater than expected.

SAI S2025-5
(i).synopsis: Asset Share Definition, CI Business Review, Duration Effects

(ii).question: Discuss why the asset share may be greater than expected on longer duration policies.  The economy in Country Z has been in recession for the last 5 years. This economic environment has placed downward pressure on premiums across all health and care insurance products. Reviewable premiums have replaced guaranteed premiums as the industry norm for new CI business sold in Country Z.
(ii).synopsis: Asset Share Variance, Guaranteed Premiums, Economic Impact

(iii).question: Explain why insurance companies may have adopted reviewable premiums for new CI business sold in Country Z.  Recently, Company A has made the following changes: • new CI business will be written exclusively on a reviewable premium basis  the investment strategy for the assets backing CI business has been updated.
(iii).synopsis: Reviewable Premiums, Industry Norms, Economic Recession

(iv).question: Justify why Company A has updated its investment strategy.  The chief investment officer of Company A has asked for additional resources to carry out the work to implement the updated investment strategy.
(iv).synopsis: Investment Strategy, Asset Backing, ALM Objectives

(v).question: Outline the tasks Company A will need to undertake as part of implementing the updated investment strategy.
(v).synopsis: Implementation Tasks, resource allocation, Investment Governance
